method_atom: A construction that defines a class of structures on a space as a common generalization unifying two previously distinct structure types, sorts the resulting structures into discrete parity classes (odd/even type), and lets a symmetry group built from the underlying automorphisms plus shifts by a closed 2-form act on them. The structures are characterized as critical points of a variational functional defined on a constrained set of closed differential forms, and the local deformation (moduli) space is shown to be an open subset of a cohomology group.
object_atom: Even-dimensional smooth manifolds, and in the special case six-dimensional ones, carrying geometric structures that generalize Calabi-Yau and symplectic manifolds.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the variational/critical-point characterization with a moduli space given by an open set in cohomology under a symmetry-group action; the domain-specific part is the differential-geometric setting of Calabi-Yau/symplectic structures on even-dimensional (notably six-dimensional) manifolds.
